creator: ale@FreeBSD.org
text:
Matthew Seaman ha scritto:
 > As discussed in the thread starting with this message:
 >
 > http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2011-November/071511.html
 >
 > a number of ports are redefining PORT_DBDIR already defined with the
 > same value in bsd.port.mk
 
 I've just read the thread, but the conclusions are not always correct: 
 once upon a time there wasn't the <bsd.port.options.mk> makefile, so to 
 load the options before including bsd.port.pre.mk you had to do the 
 trick implemented in phpX-extensions. Currently such trick is not 
 needed, so it's correct to remove it (I'm testing the patch).
 
 > there's a lot of ports that essentially set
 > LATEST_LINK to ${PKGNAMEPREFIX}${PORTNAME}${PKGNAMESUFFIX} -- and that
 > is actually the default value already.
 
 Not exaclty. As before, the setting order is important. Look at the 
 following code in bsd.port.mk:
 
 .if defined(LATEST_LINK)
 UNIQUENAME?=    ${LATEST_LINK}
 .else
 UNIQUENAME?=    ${PKGNAMEPREFIX}${PORTNAME}
 .endif
 OPTIONSFILE?=   ${PORT_DBDIR}/${UNIQUENAME}/options
 ...
 PKGBASE?=                       ${PKGNAMEPREFIX}${PORTNAME}${PKGNAMESUFFIX}
 LATEST_LINK?=           ${PKGBASE}
 
 Now it should be clear why LATEST_LINK is redefined.
 
 -- 
 Alex Dupre
